examples.title: Assignment
examples.source:
/*
 * BASIC / assignment, pre/post increment and decrement
 * Targets: ASSIGN plus the desugaring of ++/-- into ADD/SUB. Each effect is
 * captured as a number and summed.
 *
 *    7                x = 7
 * + 27                a = b = c = 9  -> a+b+c
 * + 11                post-inc:  old(5) + n(6)
 * + 12                pre-inc:   val(6) + n(6)
 * + 11                post-dec:  old(6) + n(5)
 * + 10                pre-dec:   val(5) + n(5)
 * + 20                build-up:  (0+5)*4
 * = 98
 *
 * Expected output:
 *   assignment total = 98
 */

x = 7;
a = b = c = 9;

n = 5;  pi_old = n++;  pi_n = n;     // post-inc:  5, then 6
m = 5;  pr_val = ++m;  pr_m = m;     // pre-inc:   6, then 6
p = 6;  pd_old = p--;  pd_p = p;     // post-dec:  6, then 5
q = 6;  pr2_val = --q; pr2_q = q;    // pre-dec:   5, then 5

build = 0;
build = build + 5;
build = build * 4;                   // 20

total =   x
        + (a + b + c)
        + (pi_old + pi_n)
        + (pr_val + pr_m)
        + (pd_old + pd_p)
        + (pr2_val + pr2_q)
        + build;

print("assignment total = ", total, "\n");

examples.title: Branching
Comparison
examples.source:
/*
 * BASIC / comparison
 * Targets: IF_LESS, IF_LESSEQ, IF_GREATER, IF_GREATEREQ, IF_EQ, IF_NOTEQ and the
 * nil/bool comparison rules. Each comparison is asserted in one big 'and' chain;
 * if every rule holds, the whole thing is true.
 *
 * Expected output:
 *   comparison all correct = true
 */

ok =     (3 < 5)
     and (not (5 < 3))
     and (5 <= 5)
     and (7 > 2)
     and (not (2 >= 9))
     and (4 == 4)
     and (not (4 == 5))
     and (4 != 5)
     and (nil == nil)            // nil equals only nil
     and (not (nil == 0))        // nil is not 0
     and (true == 1)             // bool coerces the other side to truthiness
     and (false == 0);

print("comparison all correct = ", ok, "\n");

examples.title: Functions
Logical
examples.source:
/*
 * BASIC / logical operators
 * Targets: 'and', 'or', 'not', and truthiness. (Short-circuit side effects are
 * covered separately by short_circuit.asc.) Everything is folded into one
 * boolean.
 *
 * Expected output:
 *   logical all correct = true
 */

ok =     (true and true)
     and (not (true and false))
     and (false or true)
     and (not (false or false))
     and (not (not true))
     and (not false)
     and (5 and true)              // nonzero number is truthy
     and ("hi" and true)           // non-empty string is truthy
     and (not (0 and true))        // 0 is falsy
     and (not ("" and true));      // "" is falsy

print("logical all correct = ", ok, "\n");

// Error: Can't have function symbol as left part of '='

// Checking for user functions
foo = print;

// Checking for library functions just in case

/*
Expected output:

examples.title: Funcdecl shadowing
examples.source:
// front3 slide21

function f(){
    // Legal: new function f at scope 1
    function f(){}
    // Error: func with same name exists in same scope
    function f(){}

}

x;

// Error: variable with same name exists in same scope
function x(){}

// Error: same with library function name
function sin(){}


/*
Expected output:

examples.title: Idlist shadowing
examples.source:
function foo(a){
    // Legal: the 2 'a's are different, no conflict
    // Legal: formal argument foo is different from function foo (like writing "local foo" inside of
    //        the body of 'bar'). This means that inside of 'bar', if user wants to refer to function
    //        'foo' they must use ::foo() (which means that they wouldn't be able to refer to it at
    //        all if it wasn't global).
    function bar(a, foo){
        // Refers to formalvar
        foo = 5; 
        // Calls function foo with formalvar foo as a parameter
        ::foo(foo);
        return true;
    }
}

// front3 slide21

// Legal: just 3 different formal variables
function f(x, y, z){}

// Error: 2nd x in same idlist
function g(x, y, x){}

// Error: libfunc as formalvar
function h(x, cos){}

LINE: 12 primary <- const

LINE: 12 expression <- term
LINE: 12 returnstmt <- RETURN expression ;

LINE: 12 statement list <- statement statement list
LINE: 13 block <- { stmt_list }

LINE: 13 statement <- function definition

LINE: 14 block <- { stmt_list }

LINE: 14 statement list <- statement
LINE: 19 funcdeclare <- FUNCTION ID

LINE: 19 idlist <- idlist , ID
LINE: 19 funcparams <- ( idlist )

LINE: 22 funcdeclare <- FUNCTION ID
LINE: 22 idlist <- ID
LINE: 22 idlist <- idlist , ID
[SYNTAX WARNING] - Attempted to redeclare Formal Argument 'x' (already exists in the same function declaration) | custom_tests/Phase2/Errors/idlist_shadowing.alpha:22:18

examples.title: Local libfunc overshadow
examples.source:
x;

{
    // Error: can't create local symbol with libfunc name
    local print;

    function foo(){
        // Legal: new variable name, no conflict as no function with same name exists in scope 2
        local foo;

    }

    // Legal: finds function foo above
    local foo;

}

// Legal: libfuncs are declared on scope 0, so, finds 'print'
local print;

examples.title: Member call as lvalue

function foo(){}

// Legal: a methodcall
foo().b..c();

// Legal: assigning value to member
foo().a = 5;

// Error: should be grammar error, call as lvalue

examples.title: Missing global
examples.source:
x;

{
    // Legal: no new symbol, references global x
    x;
    
    {
        // Legal: Finds globaly x
        ::x;

        // Error: no global y
        ::y;
    }

    // Legal: finds global libfunc
    ::input();
    
    {
        // Error: no global func
        ::Func();
    }
}

examples.title: Return no func
examples.source:
// Error: returning outside of function
return;

function foo(){
    // Legal
    return true;

    while(true){
        // Legal: empty return
        if(false) return;
    }
}


while(false){
    // Error: same as previous one, inside of loop just in case...
    return;
}

examples.title: Unreachable symbol within func
examples.source:
x;

function f(){
    // Legal: finds x, it's ok that its scope is outside the function because it is global
    x; 

    // Legal: no local symbol x, creates new local var
    local x;

    function g(a){
        // Legal: finds *global* x
        ::x;

        function h(b){
            // Error: finds x on scope 1 because it's the closest one and it is unreachable due to function(s) being in between
            x;

            // Legal: Functions are visible/callable even if there are (an)other function(s) in between 
            f();

            // Legal: b is local formalvar and reacable
            // Error: a is formal var outside of function scope so it is unreachable
            print( a + b );
        }

    }

    // Legal: finds local x in same scope (1). Just in case...
    x;
}
